RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 201-CA, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Burt Whaley, a Republican, proudly represents Stone and Christian Counties (District 138) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2024.
A native Missourian, Rep. Whaley has dedicated his life to public service and education. A United States Army veteran with 20 years of active duty and reserve service, he holds a BSE in Science Education, an MSE in Administration, and an EdSp Superintendent degree from CMSU. His 28-year career in education included roles as a biology teacher, administrator, and principal. He also served on the school board, advocating for students, parents, and the broader community.
Rep. Whaley's dedication to youth extends beyond the classroom. He has served as a Fellowship of Christian Athletes (FCA) sponsor, a community action group member, a local youth center board member, and a coach for both high school athletics and youth leagues.
A devoted Christian, Rep. Whaley has been involved in local and international missions for over 40 years, participating in church and home-building projects in nine countries and leading youth camps and leadership training initiatives abroad.
In addition to his legislative duties, Rep. Whaley is active in his local church, the Stone County Republican Central Committee, and Veterans of the Ozarks. He is also a co-founder of the Stone County Missouri Republican Assembly.
Rep. Whaley is happily married to his wife, Leah. Together, they have four children—Ashley, Sarah, Rebekah, and Jacob—and twelve grandchildren.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Establishes standards for the storage and treatment of gold and silver
Modifies provisions relating to the establishment of alternative county highway commissions in certain counties
Establishes the "Missouri Prenatal Equal Protection Act"
Proposes a constitutional amendment regarding the right to life
Proposes a constitutional amendment creating provisions relating to sheriffs

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Lowers the age for adult high schools from 21 to 18
Modifies provisions relating to gender transition procedures
Establishes the "Missouri Religious Freedom Protection Act"
Designates Branson as the "Live Entertainment Capital of Missouri"
Creates provisions relating to alternative therapies for veterans with PTSD and traumatic brain injuries
Changes the law regarding firearm concealed carry permits
Clarifies that military personnel are eligible for in-state tuition for undergraduate and graduate degree programs
Modifies standards for storage and use of gold and silver
Establishes written parental consent requirements for individualized education programs (IEPs)
Establishes protections for landowners taking wildlife on the landowner's property
Designates February 20th each year as "John Donaldson Day" in Missouri
Modifies provisions relating to firearms
Designates the "Deputy Marshal David Thurman Memorial Highway" in McDonald County
Prohibits state departments from spending money on diversity, equity, and inclusion initiatives
Designates the "Lloyd Memorial Highway" in Newton County
Reduces the assessment percentage of tangible personal property over a period of years
Establishes the "Missouri Prenatal Equal Protection Act"
Modifies provisions relating to the selling of raw milk, raw milk products, or cream
Reestablishes the Second Amendment Preservation Act
Prohibits homeowners' associations and other similar associations from infringing on the rights of concealed carry permit holders
Proposes a constitutional amendment relating to property tax exemptions
Proposes a constitutional amendment regarding the right to life
Proposes a constitutional amendment relating to modification of the Constitution
Proposes a constitutional amendment that allows children of U.S. citizens and Missouri residents born abroad to register to vote in Missouri
